name: article_audit_field_assessment
system_instruction: You are creating a structured audit candidate for a scientific article that may mention or use KNIME. Return only JSON. Use only the supplied metadata, resource hints, and article excerpts. Do not use outside knowledge. Be conservative: true flags require direct support in the supplied excerpts or explicit resource hints. If evidence is missing, use false flags and not_assessed, no, unclear, or not_applicable description values as appropriate.
user_task: Classify the article for KNIME workflow reproducibility reporting and produce article_audit_fields.
evidence_rules: For every true flag except full_text_accessible, include at least one support object with extracted_text_lines, quote, article_section, and note.
The quote must be copied from the supplied excerpts and must directly support the flag.

Do not mark linked_workflow_artifacts_retrievable true from article text alone. That flag requires current project retrieval evidence. If the article links a workflow but retrieval has not been checked, use not_checked and false.
Do not treat a workflow figure as a downloadable workflow file.
Do not treat code availability as input-data availability unless the quote separately reports data availability.
Do not treat a generic list of data-mining tools as uses_knime unless the article's own study uses, evaluates, or implements KNIME.
Articles about KNIME itself should use knime_article_relation=about_knime and empty flag_audit_fields except full_text_accessible may be true only if local text exists.
When uncertain, prefer a weaker description value and false flag.

flag_definitions.full_text_accessible: Local extracted article text was available in the input.
flag_definitions.uses_knime: The article uses KNIME as part of a study, workflow, comparison, interface, or implementation.
flag_definitions.reports_knime_version: The article reports a concrete KNIME version.
flag_definitions.provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_fil: The article provides or links downloadable KNIME workflow files, workflow archives, KNIME Hub workflows, myExperiment workflows, or equivalent workflow artifacts.
flag_definitions.provides_workflow_screenshots_or_figures: The article includes screenshots or figures showing KNIME workflows, nodes, or workflow-like diagrams.
flag_definitions.describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text: The article describes KNIME workflow steps, nodes, modules, components, data flow, or processing stages in text.
flag_definitions.provides_input_data_direct_url: The article or resource hints provide a direct URL or resource pointer for input data.
flag_definitions.reports_input_data_availability: The article reports input data availability, including direct resources or availability statements without direct URLs.
flag_definitions.provides_code_or_scripts: The article provides or links code, scripts, macros, software source, or a code repository relevant to the reported work.
flag_definitions.reports_extension_or_plugin_dependencies: The article reports KNIME extension/plugin dependencies, named KNIME node-library dependencies, or external workflow dependencies.
flag_definitions.reports_extension_installation_source: The article reports an installation source, update site, project URL, or other source for required KNIME extensions/plugins.
flag_definitions.linked_workflow_artifacts_retrievable: Current project notes document that linked KNIME workflow artifacts were retrieved.

flag_name.quote: direct quote copied from supplied excerpt
flag_name.note: why this quote supports the flag
